records.title: HOTTech: High Operating Temperature Technology program (NASA Glenn Research Center / GEER facility)
records.authors_or_org: NASA Science Mission Directorate, Planetary Science Division; NASA Glenn Research Center
records.claim_supported: NASA's HOTTech program targets technology (SiC/diamond/GaN electronics, batteries, power generation, actuators, seals) able to survive at least 60 days on the Venus surface, tested in the Glenn Extreme Environment Rig (GEER, ~900F/~90x Earth atmospheric pressure, corrosive-gas injection). December 2022-February 2023 collaborative testing produced a 30-day diamond-diode demonstration and vermiculite-gasket corrosion-resistance results. Named performer institutions confirmed on re-fetch: University of Dayton (battery technology), Arizona State University (diamond and GaN electronics), NASA Glenn Research Center (SiC memory), plus Stanford University, Penn State University, Boeing, and Makel Engineering (components), and University of Arkansas.
records.notes: Page fetched directly this session; named PI institutions and GEER facility specs confirmed from the fetched page itself. | Verifier independently re-fetched this page directly this session; performer-institution list and GEER facility specs confirmed and slightly expanded (Makel Engineering named, plus specific technology-per-institution breakdown) versus the scout's original summary.

records.title: Deep Trek High Temperature Electronics Project (DOE Award DE-FC26-03NT41834 to Honeywell)
records.authors_or_org: U.S. Department of Energy / National Energy Technology Laboratory (NETL); Honeywell International Inc. (Aerospace D&S, Plymouth, MN)
records.claim_supported: DOE's Deep Trek program (award DE-FC26-03NT41834, 2003-2007) funded Honeywell to apply SOI CMOS technology to build reliable >225C electronic components (EEPROM, FPGA, precision op-amp, sigma-delta ADC) for deep (>20,000 ft) 'smart drilling' tools -- a direct, dated, government-funded buyer/technology-development record predating the current DARPA THERMAL/ARPA-E SUPERHOT programs by two decades.
records.notes: Project metadata (award number, contractor, report date) fetched from the NETL project page this session; the PDF final report itself returned a certificate/binary-parsing issue and its narrative content (deliverables list) is drawn from a WebSearch synthesis of the same document rather than independently re-read in full -- flagged for verifier to re-fetch the PDF directly. | UNRESOLVED VERIFICATION GAP CARRIED FORWARD: project metadata (award number, contractor, report date) was fetched from the NETL project page by the scout, but the PDF final report itself returned a certificate/binary-parsing issue in the scout's session and was not re-attempted by the verifier this session given time constraints across 46 records; the deliverables-list narrative remains sourced from WebSearch synthesis of the same document, not independently re-read in full. Accepted as T1 government record on the strength of the confirmed award metadata (award number/contractor/agency/date), but the specific deliverables list should be treated as a secondary-source claim pending a direct PDF re-read.

records.title: API Standard 17F -- Subsea Production Control Systems (incorporating SIIS interface annexes)
records.authors_or_org: American Petroleum Institute (API); Subsea Instrumentation Interface Standardization (SIIS) initiative
records.claim_supported: Governs design, fabrication, testing, installation, and operation of subsea production/processing control systems (SPCS); incorporates SIIS-developed interface protocols for analog (Annex D/Level 1), digital serial (Annex E/Level 2), and Ethernet TCP/IP (Annex G/Level 3) subsea sensor communication -- relevant standards baseline for autonomous/remote subsea control electronics in harsh (high-pressure, corrosive, remote) environments.
records.notes: Standard-store listing page not independently fetched (search-snippet only, from a commercial standards reseller, not api.org itself); flagged for verifier to obtain the official API-hosted record. | URL UPDATED by verifier: raw record cited a commercial reseller listing (store.accuristech.com) that was never fetched; verifier located and fetched an official api.org-hosted PDF this session (binary content retrieved, XMP metadata readable, full body text not extractable). EDITION/YEAR AMBIGUITY FLAGGED (unresolved): WebSearch indicates a Third Edition published May 2014, a Third Edition reaffirmation dated November 1, 2017, and a separate Fourth Edition published December 2017 (with Errata 1/2 in 2018-2019). The raw record's year (2017) is plausible under either the 3rd-edition reaffirmation or the 4th edition, but which specific edition this record refers to is not resolved -- do not cite an edition number without further confirmation.

records.title: IEC 60751:2022 -- Industrial platinum resistance thermometers and platinum temperature sensors
records.authors_or_org: International Electrotechnical Commission (IEC)
records.claim_supported: Edition 3.0 (2022-01-27) of the international standard for industrial platinum resistance thermometers/temperature sensors, specifying resistance-vs-temperature requirements referencing ITS-90; a baseline reference for temperature-sensor accuracy and interchangeability specifications relevant to high-temperature downhole/industrial instrumentation.
records.notes: PDF sample fetched this session but returned unreadable binary/encoded content (not extractable text); edition/date confirmed via WebSearch snippet of the same document title, not independently read. Flag for verifier to obtain a text-readable copy. | URL UPDATED by verifier: raw record cited an unofficial PDF-sample mirror (cdn.standards.iteh.ai) that returned unreadable binary content in the scout's session. Verifier located the correct official IEC Webstore publication page (an earlier guessed publication-ID URL 404'd; the correct ID 63753 was found via WebSearch) and fetched it successfully this session, confirming edition, exact publication date, and scope directly from the standards body's own site -- a strictly stronger evidence base than the raw record had.

records.title: MIL-STD-883L Change 1 -- Test Method Standard, Microcircuits
records.authors_or_org: U.S. Department of Defense / Defense Logistics Agency (DLA)
records.claim_supported: Establishes uniform methods, controls, and procedures (environmental, mechanical, electrical, workmanship, quality) for testing monolithic/multichip/film/hybrid microcircuits for military and aerospace electronic systems; current revision is Revision L with Change 1, dated June 27, 2025.
records.notes: Official DLA ASSIST-QuickSearch page fetched directly this session, confirming current revision letter/change/date and scope statement. | Verifier independently re-fetched the official DLA ASSIST-QuickSearch page this session, confirming Revision L Change 1 dated 27-JUN-2025 matches the scout's original record exactly.

records.title: Status of the DARPA WBST High Power Electronics Program in SiC Device Development and Technology Transition
records.authors_or_org: Allen R. Hefner Jr., Sharon Beermann-Curtin (NIST)
records.claim_supported: Describes DARPA's Wide Bandgap Semiconductor Technology (WBST) High-Voltage High-Frequency SiC power program status, including plans for a prototype 2.7 MVA Solid State Power Substation (SSPS) and reliability/manufacturability qualification work -- an early (2007) precedent for the current DARPA THERMAL program's approach to SiC/GaN qualification.
records.notes: NIST publications page fetched directly this session. This is a government-technical-conference presentation summary hosted by NIST, not a peer-reviewed journal article, and is categorized as source_type government rather than academic. | Verifier independently re-fetched the NIST publications page this session, confirming title, authors, venue (GOMACTech 2007), and scope match. Categorized as source_type government (NIST-hosted publications-database record of a government-conference presentation), not academic_peer_reviewed, per scout's original correct classification -- retained.

records.title: 고온용 SiC CMOS 소자 기술 연구 (Study of the SiC CMOS device technology for high temperature applications)
records.authors_or_org: Park Geun-hyung (PI), Lee Hyung-gyu, Kim Young-seok, Kim Ki-ho (Chungbuk National University); funded by Korea Science and Engineering Foundation, Ministry of Science and Technology
records.claim_supported: Korea Science and Engineering Foundation-funded project (2002-2003) reports the first domestically fabricated Korean SiC NMOS and PMOS devices and basic logic-circuit libraries (inverters, NAND, NOR); n-channel threshold voltage fell from 7.8V at room temperature to 5.4V at 250C, attributed to SiC-SiO2 interface charge density; peak carrier mobility 36.3 cm2/V-s (NMOS) at 200C, minimum contact resistance 1.3x10^-3 ohm-cm^2 using molybdenum contacts; demonstrated inverter/NAND/NOR logic circuits.
records.notes: Official Korean government science-report repository (KISTI) fetched directly this session; a 2003 program (predating this lane's other Korean record, the 2025 Aerospace/MDPI paper) providing a KR local-language primary source with concrete device numbers. | Verifier independently re-fetched the official Korean government science-report repository (KISTI) this session, confirming title, authors, funding organization, and key numeric findings match (and adding the molybdenum-contact-resistance detail). A 2003 program providing a KR local-language primary source with concrete device numbers.

records.title: A*STAR launches industry-grade 200mm silicon carbide Open R&D Line (Innovate Together, SEMICON Southeast Asia 2025)
records.authors_or_org: Agency for Science, Technology and Research (A*STAR), Institute of Microelectronics (IME), Singapore
records.claim_supported: A*STAR launched (announced May 23, 2025, at the Innovate Together event) what it describes as the world's first industry-grade 200mm SiC Open R&D Line, consolidating materials growth, defect analysis, device fabrication, and testing under one roof; named equipment/materials partners include ASM, centrotherm, Nissin, Soitec, and Toray, with STMicroelectronics, an unnamed 'top foundry,' and local startup WaferLead as facility users -- signals Singapore state investment to close SiC wide-bandgap capability gaps versus the US/China/Japan/Korea/Taiwan.
records.notes: TIER/SOURCE_TYPE DOWNGRADED by verifier: raw record classified this as source_type=government/T1, but the actually-fetched document is a trade-press article (semiconductor-today.com), not a primary A*STAR/government page. Verifier attempted to locate and fetch A*STAR's own official press release (a-star.edu.sg) this session; the specific URL found via WebSearch returned HTTP 404. Reclassified as trade_press/T2 per SOURCE_STANDARDS ('high-quality trade press with primary links'), supported by independent corroboration across four outlets (semiconductor-today.com, EE Times, dqindia.com, Yahoo News Singapore) all describing the same primary event with consistent details.

records.title: SLB and Ormat Partner to Accelerate Integrated Geothermal Asset Development and Enhanced Geothermal Systems
records.authors_or_org: SLB; Ormat Technologies, Inc.
records.claim_supported: October 27, 2025: SLB and Ormat announced a partnership to design/construct an Enhanced Geothermal Systems (EGS) pilot at an existing Ormat site, with SLB contributing subsurface/reservoir/well-construction expertise (i.e., high-temperature downhole tool capability) and Ormat contributing power-plant design/operations, explicitly to 'de-risk' commercial-scale EGS deployment, and explicitly citing AI/data-center power demand as a driver.

records.title: Caterpillar to provide autonomous machinery to Rio Tinto (Koodaideri iron-ore project)
records.authors_or_org: Caterpillar Inc.; Rio Tinto; WesTrac
records.claim_supported: Announced May 7, 2019: Caterpillar and WesTrac agreed to supply Rio Tinto's Koodaideri iron-ore project (Pilbara, Western Australia) with 20 autonomous 793F haul trucks, 4 autonomous blast drills, plus loaders/dozers/diggers, integrated with Rio Tinto's mine-automation system as part of its 'Mine of the Future' initiative (launched 2008); Rio Tinto projected the site would reach 43 million tonnes/year production from 2021; no dollar figure disclosed.

records.title: CISSOID high-temperature semiconductor product line (CHT/CMT/CXT families)
records.authors_or_org: CISSOID SA
records.claim_supported: CISSOID offers three high-temperature product families (CHT -55 to +225C, CMT -55 to +175C, CXT -40 to +175C) across ADCs/comparators, amplifiers, DC-DC converters, SiC/GaN gate drivers (to 1700V), logic gates, oscillators, 1200V SiC/IGBT power modules (20-550A), and discrete SiC/NMOS/PMOS devices, rated 15-year life at 175C / 5-year life at 225C; states independent NASA and Sandia National Labs testing down to -195C and up to +400C. The vendor page itself now marks this product family as obsolete (last-time-buy), indicating a supply discontinuity in this market segment. No public pricing was found.
records.notes: Vendor page fetched directly this session; a vendor claim (not independently validated) per SOURCE_STANDARDS claim discipline. The 'now obsolete' status is a directly-quoted vendor statement and is itself a notable bottleneck signal for this lane. | Verifier independently re-fetched this vendor page this session, confirming all temperature ranges, lifetime figures, and the 'now obsolete' notice match exactly. Labeled a vendor claim (not independently validated) per SOURCE_STANDARDS claim discipline; the obsolescence notice is itself a notable bottleneck/supply-discontinuity signal for this lane, not merely a marketing claim.

records.title: Downhole Tools Market Report 2026 (Research and Markets)
records.authors_or_org: Research and Markets
records.claim_supported: Values the global downhole tools market at $5.79 billion in 2025, rising to $6.07 billion in 2026 (2025-2026 CAGR 4.7%), reaching $7.33 billion by 2030 (2026-2030 CAGR 4.8%), driven by unconventional-resource investment, shale drilling, enhanced oil recovery, and offshore redevelopment.
records.notes: Consultancy market-size estimate, T3 by SOURCE_STANDARDS definition; not fetched this session (listing page only, no purchase). See L15-046 for a conflicting estimate from a different consultancy for the same market/year -- an unresolved contradiction, not averaged. | Consultancy market-size estimate, T3 by SOURCE_STANDARDS definition -- kept in the ledger per explicit instruction to preserve both conflicting T3 downhole-tools market reports rather than reject one for access difficulty (T3 sources are context-only and capped at 15% of the accepted ledger, not held to the same fetch rigor as T1). UNRESOLVED CONFLICT PRESERVED: disagrees with L15-046 (Fortune Business Insights) by roughly 30-40% on 2025/2026 base-year figures ($5.79B/$6.07B here vs. $4.10B/$4.31B there) despite both claiming to cover the same 'downhole tools' market and both citing a similar $7.33B 2030 endpoint -- the underlying market-definition scope is not reconciled and the conflict is shown, not averaged. SOURCE-VERIFIER UPDATE (2026-07-12): L15-046 (Fortune Business Insights) was rejected this session as unfetchable (fortunebusinessinsights.com returned HTTP 403 on every route tried: report landing page, alternate /industry-reports/ path, bare-domain path, and companion press release). Its claim (~$4.10B 2025 / ~$4.31B 2026 / $7.33B by 2030) is no longer in the accepted ledger, but the ~40% disagreement with this record's $5.79B/$6.07B/$7.33B figures for the same nominal market is preserved here as an open flag: even a single accepted T3 estimate for 'downhole tools market size' should be treated as a wide-uncertainty-band placeholder, not a precise figure, given the magnitude of divergence observed between the two consultancy reports that were both live this session.

records.notes: MAJOR CORRECTION: scout's source (a greyb.com blog aggregator, not independently fetched) described this as a finalized 'partnership' for deployment 'across US tribal reservations' and classified it as demand_evidence_type procurement_award. Verifier located and fetched Amber Kinetics' own primary press release this session, which reveals the actual transaction is a non-binding Letter of Support plus a single commissioned unit -- not a binding multi-site procurement contract. url/canonical_key updated to the primary source; source_type upgraded from trade_press to vendor_datasheet (company's own release); demand_evidence_type downgraded from procurement_award to none since a non-binding letter of support is not primary procurement evidence. Date corrected to August 6, 2025 (scout said 'reported October 2025', which appears to conflate this with unrelated October 2025 secondary blog coverage).

records.title: HIWIN Mikrosystem Corp. -- direct-drive linear motor and motion-control product line
records.authors_or_org: HIWIN Mikrosystem Corp. (Taiwan)
records.claim_supported: HIWIN, a Taiwan-headquartered motion-control manufacturer, produces Permanent Magnet Linear Synchronous Motors (PMLSM) in iron-core (up to 4,458 N peak standard / 20,827 N peak water-cooled) and ironless (up to 4,012 N peak) variants for semiconductor, biotech, automation, and machine-tool applications.
records.notes: Not independently fetched this session; market-position claims (No. 2 globally) are the vendor's own self-description and should be treated as a vendor claim, not independently validated market-share data. Included primarily for Taiwan geographic coverage, which is otherwise absent from this lane's dataset. | CORRECTION: the scout's claim that HIWIN markets itself as 'No. 2 globally' in ball-screw/linear-guideway technology was NOT present on the specific page fetched this session (which covers only linear motors, no ranking claim, no direct-drive-motor content either); that ranking claim is unverified this session and has been removed from claim_supported pending confirmation elsewhere. Retained the record and technical specs, which the fetched page does support. Included primarily for Taiwan geographic coverage.
